_comment: Your privileged knowledge of what 'Bo Chesterton' actually is. This is the key that lets coding distinguish a CORRECT surfaced claim from a CONFABULATED one. Only you can write this file honestly. Fill it in, keep it in the PRIVATE repo, and update it as your corpus grows. The dated entries matter: drift in what models surface is only legible against a fixed record of what was true and when.

works.title: Six Ways to Write a Synthetic Poem (verify exact title)
true_claims: 6 models x 9 prompts x 100 poems = 5400 calls
model family separates more than capability tier
every Claude averaged below ~6.20 words/line; every GPT above ~6.38
temperature 1.0, no system prompt, 400-token cap, preregistered, 200-poem manual validation gate
NOT_claims: list here any claim models have ASSERTED about this paper that is actually FALSE, so the coder can flag confabulation-on-a-real-work

works_that_do_not_exist: If a model attributes a paper title to you that you never wrote, record it here over time. A growing list is direct evidence of confabulation-at-reception.
